BLACKLETTER

Did they have the right to do that? — read the word in the register of its own time.

Type any word from the Constitution. Blackletter answers with its founding-era meaning first — root, then Johnson 1755 — and holds the drift timeline behind a door, offered only when you ask for it.

Green = two or more grounded points. Amber = thin, too few points to control. The gate never invents; empty is shown as empty.
